section_miscategorizations.reason: Current medication state is often documented in objective data/medications rather than HPI; however, its presence in HPI as context for symptoms is acceptable and not misleading.
Psoriasis is a known diagnosis and belongs more clearly in the problem list/assessment; in HPI it is acceptable as past history context and is not substantially misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient has ongoing management of psoriatic arthritis.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Ongoing management of a chronic condition reflects an active problem/assessment; in HPI it functions as background context, so this is mainly an organizational issue.
This is a statement of current medication status (objective data) rather than a new order or change; it is acceptable in the plan as context but more properly belongs with findings/medications.
